Question: What is the current global prevalence of diabetes mellitus (2023)?
Explanation: IDF Atlas 10th Edition reports 537 million adults with diabetes globally

Question: According to ADA 2023 guidelines, which HbA1c level confirms diabetes diagnosis?
Explanation: ADA defines diabetes as HbA1c ≥ 6.5% on two occasions

Question: First-line pharmacological treatment for Type 2 diabetes according to ADA/EASD 2022 consensus is:
Explanation: Metformin remains first-line unless contraindicated

Question: In India, approximately what percentage of adults aged 20-79 years have diabetes?
Explanation: 77 million out of 500 million adults 20-79 years, approximately 15.3%

Question: Which of the following best describes the pathophysiology of Type 2 diabetes mellitus?
Option2: Insulin resistance combined with relative insulin deficiency
Option3: Complete absence of insulin secretion due to genetic mutation
Option4: Pancreatic damage due to chronic pancreatitis
Explanation: Type 2 DM involves both insulin resistance and progressive β-cell dysfunction
Reference: Guyton & Hall Textbook of Medical Physiology

Question: According to ADA guidelines, pre-diabetes is diagnosed when fasting plasma glucose is:
Explanation: IFG: 100-125 mg/dL, IGT in OGTT: 140-199 mg/dL

Question: Which of the following is NOT a classic symptom of diabetes mellitus?
Explanation: Polycythemia is increased red blood cells, not a diabetes symptom

Question: The gold standard diagnostic test for diabetes mellitus is:
Explanation: OGTT is the gold standard as it assesses glucose metabolism under standardized conditions

Question: Which complication screening test detects early diabetic nephropathy?
Explanation: UACR detects microalbuminuria, earliest sign of diabetic nephropathy

Question: Which modifiable risk factor has the strongest association with Type 2 diabetes?
Explanation: Obesity is the strongest modifiable risk factor, doubling diabetes risk

Question: According to WHO "Best Buys" for diabetes prevention, which intervention is cost-effective at population level?
Explanation: Fiscal policies like sugar taxation are highly cost-effective for diabetes prevention

Question: The Diabetes Prevention Program (DPP) trial showed that intensive lifestyle intervention reduced diabetes risk by:
Explanation: DPP trial showed 58% reduction in diabetes incidence with lifestyle intervention over 3 years

Question: Which waist circumference cutoff is appropriate for Asian Indians according to WHO/IDF?
Option1: > 80 cm in women, > 90 cm in men
Option2: > 90 cm in women, > 100 cm in men
Option4: > 102 cm in women, > 88 cm in men
Explanation: Lower cutoffs for South Asians: >80 cm women, >90 cm men

Question: Gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) increases the lifetime risk of developing Type 2 diabetes by:
Explanation: Women with GDM have 5-7 fold increased risk of developing Type 2 DM

Question: Which medication class has been shown to reduce cardiovascular mortality in Type 2 diabetes?
Explanation: SGLT2 inhibitors like empagliflozin reduce CV mortality in EMPA-REG trial

Question: Tight glycemic control (HbA1c < 6.5%) is recommended for which group of diabetic patients?
Option4: All diabetic patients regardless of age
Explanation: Individualized targets - tighter control for younger patients without CV disease

Question: Which insulin regimen is recommended for newly diagnosed Type 2 diabetes patients with HbA1c > 10%?
Explanation: Start with single daily basal insulin, titrate gradually

Question: The most common chronic complication of diabetes mellitus is:
Explanation: Diabetic peripheral neuropathy affects 60-70% of patients

Question: Screening for diabetic retinopathy should begin:
Option1: At diagnosis for Type 1 DM, after 5 years for Type 2 DM
Option2: At diagnosis for both Type 1 and Type 2 DM
Option3: After 5 years of diabetes for both types
Option4: After puberty for Type 1 DM, at diagnosis for Type 2 DM
Explanation: Annual screening from diagnosis for both types

Question: Under NPCDCS 2010, annual diabetes screening is recommended for:
Option3: All adults above 25 years with risk factors
Explanation: NPCDCS recommends annual screening for all adults ≥30 years
Reference: Ghosh A et al. NPCDCS Guidelines

Question: The role of ASHA (Accredited Social Health Activist) in diabetes care includes:
Explanation: ASHAs create awareness, ensure follow-up, refer patients, not prescribe/diagnose

Question: Which national program provides free oral hypoglycemics for diagnosed diabetics?
Option2: NPCDCS (National Program for Cancer, Diabetes, CVD)
Explanation: NPCDCS provides free OHA and insulin through government network

Question: First-line treatment for hypoglycemia (blood glucose < 70 mg/dL) in a conscious patient is:
Explanation: Rule of 15: 15g fast-acting carbs, wait 15 minutes, recheck

Question: Classic presentation of diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) includes all EXCEPT:
Explanation: DKA presents with hyperglycemia, acidosis, ketones; may have hyponatremia from osmotic diuresis
Reference: Kitabchi AE et al. DKA Management

Question: According to ADA guidelines, adults with diabetes should perform at least how much moderate-intensity exercise per week?
Explanation: ADA recommends 150 minutes moderate intensity + muscle strengthening 2x/week
